name: Braised Celery
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim root ends off the celery and remove top leaves (save them for the soup pot).
2. Trim each stalk to about 7 inches. Scrape off tough fibers on the outer ribs. Cut each stalk in half lengthwise and rinse well.
3. In a large saucepan, bring 3 quarts of water to boiling. Add celery and cook for 10 minutes. Drain well.
4. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
5. Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and carrot and sauté for 5 minutes.
6. Transfer the onions, carrots, and melted butter to a 13x9-inch baking dish.
7. In the same skillet, melt the remaining butter. Add celery and sauté about 5 minutes until lightly browned.
8. Arrange the celery in a single layer in the baking dish with the cut side up. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
9. Pour the chicken broth and bay leaf into the skillet and heat to boiling. Pour over the celery.
10. Bake for 30 minutes or until tender. Remove the bay leaf before serving (the dish may be prepared up to this point, covered, and stored at room temperature for up to 6 hours). Place in a preheated oven at 250°F (120°C) for up to 1 hour if preparing in advance.
